summary: PR #2637 marked src/melee/gm/gmopening.c as Matching and adjusted small-data/BSS/data symbol layout metadata and declarations so the full link aligned, specifically around gmopening-owned .sbss symbols referenced externally through SDA.

key_files.role: Changed gmopening.c from NonMatching to Matching in the object list.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: configure.py hunk around MatchingFor, Object(NonMatching, "melee/gm/gmopening.c") -> Object(Matching, "melee/gm/gmopening.c")

key_files.role: Reordered and changed linkage of .sbss declarations around gm_804D67EC, gm_804D67F0, and gm_804D67F4.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: gm_804D67F4 needed to be non-static and gm_804D67EC/gm_804D67F0/gm_804D67F4 needed target order

key_files.role: Adjusted data, bss, sdata, and sdata2 symbol names, scopes, labels, and local/global metadata around gm, item, and ftKb_Init entries.
evidence_refs: changed_files: config/GALE01/symbols.txt added 28 deleted 26 across 7 hunks
raw_diff: symbols.txt hunks for gm_803DBF78, @191, @192, @201, ...bss.0, it_804D54E8, ftKb_Init_804D9368, gm_804DAAA8, it_804DD068

what_changed.text: gmopening.c was linked as matching by changing its configure.py object status from NonMatching to Matching.
The .sbss declaration order/linkage in gmopening.c was changed: gm_804D67F4 moved before gm_804D67F0 and gm_804D67EC in source order and became non-static, while gm_804D67EC moved after gm_804D67F0.
The PR body identifies the failure mode as a full-link mismatch from an external SDA reference to gm_804D67EC after gmopening.c was marked matching.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: full-link mismatch came from external SDA reference to gm_804D67EC

what_changed.text: symbols.txt received several metadata corrections, including converting some address-style global names to local compiler-style labels, adding local section labels, and changing local/global scope annotations.
The bot report recorded linked code +2164 bytes and linked data +744 bytes for GALE01, with 'No changes' in the report body.

smart_moves.text: The fix targeted the owner of the small-data symbols rather than compensating at the external reference site: the PR body says gm_804D67EC is owned by gmopening.c and the source change adjusted gmopening.c declarations.
The PR treated full-link small-data offsets as part of matching, not just individual object text matching.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: right symbol name but wrong final small-data offset

smart_moves.text: The symbols.txt changes narrowed several ordinary constants/strings/floats to local labels where the diff shows no global ownership retained.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: symbols.txt changes from it_804D54E8/it_804D54F0, ftKb_Init_804D9368..804D9380, gm_804DAAA8..804DAACC, it_804DD068..804DD078 to @ labels

decomp_lessons.lesson: When marking a TU matching, verify full-link small-data layout for symbols that may be referenced from other TUs; an external SDA reference can use the right symbol name but still land at the wrong final small-data offset if the owner TU's .sbss order or linkage is wrong.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: external SDA reference to gm_804D67EC and wrong final small-data offset

decomp_lessons.lesson: For small-data layout fixes, declaration order and static vs non-static linkage can affect final layout and may be necessary matching inputs.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: gm_804D67F4 needed to be non-static for compiler/linker layout
raw_diff: gm_804D67F4 changed from static to non-static and reordered

decomp_lessons.lesson: Symbol metadata changes can be part of a matching/link fix when data symbol size, local labels, scope, and padding influence final layout.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: data symbol size and final padding both match

naming_conventions.text: The diff uses compiler-style local labels such as @191, @192, @201, @355, and @356 for local string/data objects instead of global address-style names when global ownership is not retained.
A local float block at .data:0x803DBF78 was renamed from gm_803DBF78 to unused_floats and given an explicit ...data.0 local label.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Use owner-TU .sbss declaration order and linkage to control final SDA offsets when a full-link mismatch appears after enabling Matching.
Update symbols.txt scope/label metadata rather than inventing C storage when section ownership and local labels need to reflect layout.

follow_up_queries: full-link mismatch small-data offset Matching object
symbols.txt local @ labels scope global string floats

notes: Changed files, PR identity, merge status, and main diff hunks are directly available.
No review comments or human reviewer feedback are present in the loaded slice.

The PR body says a named data symbol gm_803DBFCC[] = "MvOpen.mth" was added, but the loaded diff excerpt for symbols.txt shows gm_803DBFCC renamed to local @201 and the source diff only shows the .sbss declaration hunk; treat the exact source-level data-symbol addition as not fully visible in the loaded diff.
The bot report provides linked code/data deltas and says 'No changes', but no build log, objdiff, or full regression output is loaded beyond that comment.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Full-link SDA references can expose owner-TU .sbss layout mistakes
accepted_candidate_records.text: When a TU is moved to Matching, check full-link small-data offsets for externally referenced owner-TU symbols. PR #2637 reports gm_804D67EC was referenced externally by fn_801A1498; after gmopening.c was marked matching, the symbol name resolved but the final SDA offset was wrong until gmopening.c's .sbss order/linkage was adjusted.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Declaration order and linkage can be deliberate layout controls for .sbss
accepted_candidate_records.text: In gmopening.c, gm_804D67F4 was reordered and made non-static, and gm_804D67EC was moved after gm_804D67F0, to align the target .sbss layout described in the PR body.

source_update_candidates.title: Mention full-link small-data offset checks when marking TUs Matching
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding an explicit note to data-sections-and-tu-splits or verification-and-regression-ledger: marking a TU Matching can perturb .sbss/.sdata layout visible to external SDA references, so full-link symbol offsets should be checked when the TU owns small-data symbols referenced elsewhere.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: external SDA reference to gm_804D67EC
source_update_candidates.reason: The existing standards cover section placement and verification generally, but this PR provides a concrete recurring pattern: owner-TU small-data layout may only fail at full link after Matching promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: gmopening.c owns gm_804D67EC/gm_804D67F0/gm_804D67F4 .sbss layout
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2637 states gm_804D67EC is owned by gmopening.c and fixes the target .sbss order around gm_804D67EC, gm_804D67F0, and gm_804D67F4; gm_804D67F4 needed non-static linkage for layout.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a path-specific ownership/layout fact from the PR, suitable for curator review rather than automatic promotion.

rejection_notes: Do not infer reviewer intent; there were no loaded review comments.

Do not promote the exact claim that gm_803DBFCC[] was added in source as a confirmed code fact from this slice alone, because the loaded source diff does not show that addition and symbols.txt shows the address as local @201.
Do not treat broad item or ftKb_Init symbol metadata changes as semantically explained; the slice only shows metadata edits, not rationale for each non-gm symbol.
